# Image Labeling Guide

How to label and annotate container images so OCIDex can ingest them reliably and display rich metadata.

---

## How OCIDex reads image metadata

OCIDex reads image metadata through two separate mechanisms:

**At ingest time (synchronous):** When an SBOM is generated by Syft (via registry scan, webhook, or a Syft-generated SBOM uploaded directly), OCIDex reads `syft:image:*` properties embedded in the BOM. Syft sources these from the image **config labels** — `LABEL` instructions in your Dockerfile. Syft does not read manifest annotations.

**After ingest (async):** The OCI enricher contacts the registry directly and reads both **manifest annotations** and **config labels**, with manifest annotations taking priority.

The practical consequence: **config labels are the authoritative source for ingest**. Manifest-only annotations will not be seen until after the async enricher runs, and they cannot rescue a rejected ingest.

---

## Mandatory fields for container SBOMs

OCIDex rejects container SBOMs with 422 if any of these three fields cannot be resolved:

| Field | What it represents |
|---|---|
| `subject_version` | Human-readable image version (e.g. `v1.4.2`) |
| `architecture` | Target CPU architecture (e.g. `amd64`, `arm64`) |
| `build_date` | When the image was built (RFC3339) |

**Architecture** is almost always handled automatically. Docker and buildkit set the config blob `architecture` field to match the build target, and Syft reads it directly.

**`subject_version` and `build_date` must be set explicitly** as config labels. Without them, ingest fails.

---

## Recommended Dockerfile labels

```dockerfile
ARG VERSION=dev
ARG BUILDTIME

LABEL org.opencontainers.image.version="$VERSION"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.created="$BUILDTIME"

# Optional but indexed by OCIDex
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.title="my-service"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.description="Short description of what this image does"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.source="https://github.com/myorg/myrepo"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.revision="$GIT_SHA"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.vendor="My Org"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.licenses="Apache-2.0"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.url="https://myorg.example.com/my-service"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.documentation="https://myorg.example.com/my-service/docs"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.authors="team@myorg.example.com"
```

Build with:

```bash
docker buildx build \
  --build-arg VERSION=v1.4.2 \
  --build-arg BUILDTIME=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) \
  --build-arg GIT_SHA=$(git rev-parse HEAD) \
  -t myregistry.example.com/my-service:v1.4.2 \
  --push .
```

---

## Multi-arch builds

For multi-arch images, build each platform separately with per-platform digest references. The index tag is used only for human convenience; OCIDex ingests each platform's manifest independently.

```bash
docker buildx build \
  --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 \
  --build-arg VERSION=v1.4.2 \
  --build-arg BUILDTIME=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) \
  -t myregistry.example.com/my-service:v1.4.2 \
  --push .
```

OCIDex's registry catalog scan will expand the image index and create a separate SBOM for each platform manifest, each with its own architecture.

---

## Manifest annotations

Manifest annotations (set via `--annotation` in buildx, or by tools like `oras`) are read by the async OCI enricher after ingest. They are **not** read by Syft, so they do not affect whether an ingest succeeds.

They are useful for updating metadata after the fact or for tools that cannot set config labels, but they are not a substitute for config labels on the ingest path.

If you set both, manifest annotations take priority in the enricher's output (which is what appears in the UI once enrichment completes).

---

## Label reference

All labels are optional except `version` and `created` for the reasons described above.

| Label | OCIDex field | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `org.opencontainers.image.version` | `imageVersion`, `subject_version` | **Required.** Human-readable version tag. |
| `org.opencontainers.image.created` | `build_date` | **Required.** RFC3339 timestamp (e.g. `2024-03-15T10:30:00Z`). |
| `org.opencontainers.image.title` | `title` | Short display name. |
| `org.opencontainers.image.description` | `description` | One-line description. |
| `org.opencontainers.image.source` | `sourceUrl` | URL to source repository. |
| `org.opencontainers.image.revision` | `revision` | Git commit SHA or tag. |
| `org.opencontainers.image.vendor` | `vendor` | Organization or team name. |
| `org.opencontainers.image.licenses` | `licenses` | SPDX expression (e.g. `Apache-2.0`). |
| `org.opencontainers.image.url` | `url` | Project or product homepage. |
| `org.opencontainers.image.documentation` | `documentation` | Docs URL. |
| `org.opencontainers.image.authors` | `authors` | Contact info. |
| `org.opencontainers.image.base.name` | `baseName` | Base image name (e.g. `debian:bookworm-slim`). |
| `org.opencontainers.image.base.digest` | `baseDigest` | Digest of the base image. |

Legacy `org.label-schema.*` labels are also supported as fallbacks but OCI standard labels are preferred.

---

## Direct API upload

If you upload a Syft-generated SBOM directly via `POST /api/v1/sbom` without the config labels above, you can supply the missing fields as query parameters:

```bash
syft myregistry.example.com/my-service@sha256:abc123 -o cyclonedx-json | \
  curl -X POST "https://ocidex.example.com/api/v1/sbom?version=v1.4.2&architecture=amd64&build_date=2024-03-15T10:30:00Z" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d @-
```

Query parameters override any values extracted from the BOM and are the most reliable option when labels are not present.
